steps.title: Show z = w from the figure
steps.content: The figure marks a right angle at $A$ and a right angle at $D$, so $AD$ is the altitude from the right-angle vertex of right triangle $ABC$ to the hypotenuse $BC$. In this altitude-to-hypotenuse setup, the smaller right triangles formed are similar to $\triangle ABC$, which forces $\angle CAD = \angle ABD$. So $z = w$.

steps.title: Find sin w from cos w
steps.content: Given $\cos w^\circ = \frac{9}{41}$, use $\sin^2 w^\circ + \cos^2 w^\circ = 1$: $$\sin^2 w^\circ = 1 - \frac{81}{1681} = \frac{1600}{1681}, \quad \sin w^\circ = \frac{40}{41}.$$

steps.title: Compute tan z
steps.content: Since $z = w$: $$\tan z^\circ = \tan w^\circ = \frac{\sin w^\circ}{\cos w^\circ} = \frac{40/41}{9/41} = \frac{40}{9}.$$ That matches the accepted answer — 40/9 (or 4.444) is correct.
